story.title: Cart badge shows stale item count after removing an item
story.description: Regression fix: the header cart badge must decrement immediately when an item is removed, hide when the cart is empty, and stay correct across navigation — it currently shows a cached pre-removal count until a page reload.

acceptance_criteria: Given a cart with N items, when the user removes one item from the cart page, then the header badge updates to N-1 without a page reload.
Given the user removes the last remaining item, when the cart becomes empty, then the header badge is hidden entirely (not shown as '0').
Given the user removes an item, when they navigate to another page and back, then the badge still shows the correct count (the fix must not depend on a reload to 'correct itself').

risks.description: The badge keeps showing a stale count after removal, misleading the user about cart contents at the checkout decision point.
An emptied cart still shows a badge (e.g. '0' or the last count), implying items remain.
